title: V12_HEBBIAN ablation verification bypass

timeline.event: V12_HEBBIAN criterion added in commit 086725bf (feat: V13-V18 implemented)
V12 ablation test passes all runs including the (untested) no_hebbian scenario; no failures detected for 15 days
DD62 deep-dive audit (docs/hypotheses/dd/DD62.md:183-186) flags the V12 path as broken: change_ratio 1.67-9.75x at threshold 0.05 means any engine trivially passes; no actual ablation is exercised
Codebase scan promotes the finding to P0 (URGENT_ACTION_LIST.md#1) and the incident is opened (docs/incident-2026-04-16-verification-bypass.md)

Decisions D8-D11 + D16 ACCEPTED (DECISION_LOG.csv): mark V12 rubber_stamp/audit=BUG, raise threshold to >= 1.0, add adversarial kill-switch, require adversarial FAIL scenario per rubber_stamp criterion, pass factory-ablated instance into verification path

Fix landed in ready/bench/bench.py:_verify_hebbian (worktree agent-a64696ce). Hexa stub bench/bench.hexa updated with the new contract. config/verification.json + ready/config/verification.json updated: V12 promoted rubber_stamp -> genuine_hard, audit=FIXED, known_bugs entry moved to fixed_bugs.

root_cause.summary: The V12 ablation test ran a single baseline-only path with no adversarial scenario, so coupling_change_ratio always reflected baseline behavior and the test could not detect a broken ablation. The threshold (0.05) was also too lenient — observed ratios 1.67-9.75x meant any engine trivially passed.
details: _verify_hebbian invoked engine_factory(cells, dim, hidden) once and measured coupling drift after 200 steps of repeated stimulus.
There was no `no_hebbian` scenario, no factory-ablated engine instance, and no kill-switch assertion that the ablation actually disables Hebbian.

Even if a fresh ConsciousnessEngine had been constructed (as DD62 originally claimed), it would still have a non-None _coupling field — the same Hebbian update path would run and produce the same drift, making the bypass invisible.

Threshold 0.05 was chosen for a much smaller observed range than what the engine actually produces; the SSOT consciousness_laws.json had already raised it to 1.0 but the criterion description in verification.json still advertised 0.05, so neither value was load-bearing for the test outcome.
root_cause.anti_pattern: Single-scenario verification of a feature whose entire safety claim depends on the difference between scenarios. An ablation test that never ablates is rubber-stamp by construction.

files_changed.change: Refactored into _run_hebbian_scenario(ablate: bool) helper + two-scenario _verify_hebbian. Baseline runs Hebbian on, no_hebbian path monkey-patches the factory-produced engine's _hebbian_update to a no-op and restores _coupling each step (defensive freeze). Test PASSES only if base_ratio >= 1.0 AND abl_ratio < 1.0.
Stub updated with the new contract spec, decision references, and explicit warning against constructing a fresh ConsciousnessEngine on the verification path.

V12_HEBBIAN: tier rubber_stamp -> genuine_hard, audit BUG -> FIXED 2026-04-16, description+test reflect two-scenario contract. tiers.rubber_stamp.criteria removes V12, tiers.genuine_hard.criteria adds V12. known_bugs entry removed; fixed_bugs section added with incident reference.
Row 1 status OPEN -> RESOLVED, resolution column populated.

test_strategy.baseline_scenario: engine_factory(cells, dim, hidden) -> 200 steps repeated stimulus -> coupling delta vs init -> change_ratio >= 1.0 required
test_strategy.no_hebbian_scenario: engine_factory(cells, dim, hidden) -> monkey-patch ce._hebbian_update to no-op via types.MethodType + freeze ce._coupling restoration each step -> 200 steps -> change_ratio < 1.0 required
test_strategy.kill_switch: passed = base_ok AND abl_ok. If a future regression re-enables Hebbian on the no_hebbian path, abl_ratio jumps back to ~1.10 and the test fails immediately.
test_strategy.fairness: torch.manual_seed(42) re-seeded between scenarios so both start from identical RNG state.

threshold.ssot: consciousness_laws.json#verify_hebbian_change_ratio_min (already 1.0; the 0.05 was a stale criterion-description value in verification.json)

kill_switch_validation.note: Verified by monkey-patching _run_hebbian_scenario to ignore the ablate flag — passed flips to False as expected, proving the kill-switch fires.

lessons: Ablation tests must run BOTH baseline and ablated scenarios and assert the difference, not the absolute value of either.
Every rubber_stamp criterion needs an adversarial FAIL scenario that the test would FAIL if the ablation path is broken — D11.
Thresholds in verification.json criterion descriptions must match the SSOT (consciousness_laws.json) — desync hides bugs by making it unclear which value is load-bearing.
Factory contracts must be honored: never construct a fresh underlying engine on a verification path that is supposed to test factory-controlled ablation — D16.

Single-scenario verification of a feature whose value depends entirely on a between-scenario delta is rubber-stamp by construction. Promote such criteria to genuine_hard only after the kill-switch lands.

preventive_actions.description: Codify D11 (adversarial FAIL scenario per rubber_stamp criterion) as a CI lint that walks shared/config/verification.json and rejects any rubber_stamp entry whose test definition does not contain an adversarial assertion.

Audit V2_NO_SPEAK_CODE, V4_PERSISTENCE, V6_SPONTANEOUS_SPEECH, V8_MITOSIS, V11_DIVERSITY for the same anti-pattern. Each must either gain an adversarial scenario or be promoted/demoted with explicit audit notes.
When porting bench.py -> bench.hexa, the hexa verify_hebbian stub must be wired to runtime with the same two-scenario contract before V12 can be considered fully hexa-native.
